utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: it's good to see you too . so i know the nurse told you about dax , i'd like to tell dax a little bit about you .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: james is a 57 year old male with a past medical history significant for coronary artery disease and lupus , who presents today with joint pain . so james , what's what's going on ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: yeah . i've been putting a , a few more miles on the bike each day and , uh , my knees have been killing me , just more so in the last three or four days or so .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . is it one knee versus the other , or both ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and , um , does it hurt more in the morning when you wake up , or does it hurt more when you're walking around and that type of thing ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: usually when i get up in the morning , they're pretty stiff and sore , and then by the end of the day , i start to feel that same sensation . kind of pretty much , like , uh , probably around dinner time or so .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and any other , any other joints bothering you , your elbows , wrists , shoulders , anything like that ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: just the knees . and have you noticed any swelling of your knees , or any redness of your knees ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: uh , they look a little inflamed in the morning , and then in the afternoon , same but not as bad , not as noticeable .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and do you have any other symptoms with this ? do you have any other associated fatigue or fever or anything like that ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , no . just , uh , repetitive motion , riding the bike the everyday , it , it's part of my workout , i just probably , uh , probably doubled the miles .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay , all right . well , tell me a little bit about . uh , you know , it's been a while since i've seen you . you know , in terms of your heart disease , and i know you got the stent placed last year .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: um , i know they took you off the plavix , are you still taking your aspirin every day ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and how are you doing with your crestor ? any problems with that ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . and any chest pain or shortness of breath ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right , and in terms of your lupus , we have , you know , this is something that was kinda back in the , in the background for you , and we have n't had to do a whole lot . you know , your kidney function's been really good . um , any , any issues as far as you know ?

utterances.speaker: patient
utterances.text: no , nothing , not at least in the last year or so .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . i think we , we might have to look into it a little bit further with this knee pain that you're having .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: but i wan na go ahead and , um ... you know , i know that the nurse did a review of system sheet and we just covered a lot here .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: any other symptoms , abdominal pain , nausea , vomiting ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: okay . all right . well , let's go ahead and do a quick physical exam .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey , dragon , show me the vital signs . so your vital signs here in the office look really good .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: there's no fever , your blood pressure and your heart rate look great , so that's , that's awesome .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: um , i'm just gon na check out your heart and your lungs .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: and your knees , and i'll let you know what i find , okay ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right . so on physical examination of your heart , you know , i do hear that's , like , two out of six systolic ejection murmur , but we've heard that in the past . your lungs sound nice and clear . and on your knee exam , i do appreciate , you know , some bilateral , uh , edema and some slight erythema , there is a slight effusion present on the right knee . does that hurt ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right , so pain to palpation of the right knee . um , and decreased range of motion of the knees bilaterally . so i just wan na look at some of your results , okay ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey , dragon . show me the right knee x-ray . so this is an x-ray of your right knee and in reviewing the results of that , everything looks fine , there's no boney abnormality , there's no fracture , which is not surprising , based on the history , okay ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey , dragon . show me the ekg . and this is your most recent ekg which we did before you came in , and that all looks really good . so , um , so no concerns from a heart standpoint . so let's talk a little bit about , you know , my assessment and my plan for you .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: so for your first problem , your , your knee pain , you know , i'm concerned that it might be from , you know , a flareup of your lupus , so i wan na go ahead and order and autoimmune panel to kinda see where we stand , okay ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: hey , dragon . order an autoimmune panel . for your next problem , the coronary artery disease , i think you're doing really well , your ekg looks good , your vital signs look good .

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: i'm gon na go ahead and order a lipid panel and another echocardiogram , okay ?

utterances.speaker: doctor
utterances.text: all right , well , the nurse will be in soon and she'll get you checked out , okay ?

detail_list.summary: The patient is a 57-year-old male with a history of coronary artery disease and lupus presenting with joint pain.
Joint pain
The patient reports bilateral knee pain for 3 to 4 days associated with increased cycling.
The doctor asks if the pain is in one knee or both.
The patient reports equal pain in both knees.
The doctor asks about the timing of the knee pain.
The patient reports stiffness and soreness in the morning and at the end of the day.
The doctor asks if other joints are affected.
The patient denies pain in other joints.
The doctor asks about swelling or redness in the knees.
The patient reports inflammation in the morning that decreases by the afternoon.
The doctor asks about associated symptoms like fatigue or fever.
The patient denies fatigue or fever.
The doctor asks if there was any injury.
The patient denies injury and attributes pain to repetitive motion from cycling.
Coronary artery disease
The doctor asks if the patient is taking daily aspirin.
The patient confirms daily aspirin use.
The doctor asks about Crestor usage.
The patient reports no problems with Crestor.
The doctor asks about chest pain or shortness of breath.
The patient denies chest pain or shortness of breath.
Lupus
The doctor asks if there are any issues related to lupus.
The patient denies lupus symptoms in the last year.
The doctor asks about abdominal pain, nausea, or vomiting.
The patient denies abdominal pain, nausea, or vomiting.
The patient's vital signs are normal.
Physical exam shows a 2/6 systolic ejection murmur, clear lungs, bilateral knee edema and erythema, right knee effusion, and decreased range of motion.
The patient reports pain upon palpation of the right knee.
Right knee X-ray is unremarkable with no bony abnormalities or fractures.
EKG is stable and unremarkable.
The doctor will order an autoimmune panel to evaluate for a lupus flare.
The doctor will order a lipid panel and an echocardiogram.
